sql: SELECT id, order_no, user_id, status, amount, created_at, updated_at FROM orders o AND status = #{status} AND o.status = #{orderStatus} AND created_at >= #{startDate} AND created_at <= #{endDate} LIMIT #{pageSize} OFFSET #{offset}
templateSql:
SELECT <include refid="orderWithCommonColumns" />
        FROM orders o
        <where>
            <include refid="orderSearchCondition" />
        </where>
        <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.paginationCondition" />

sql: SELECT * FROM orders AND status = '1' AND type = #{type} AND status = '0' AND status IN ('0', '1') AND status = #{orderStatus}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM orders
        <where>
            <if test="useCommonCondition">
                <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.complexCommonCondition" />
            </if>
            <if test="orderStatus != null">AND status = #{orderStatus}</if>
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM orders AND status = #{status} AND created_at >= #{startDate} AND created_at <= #{endDate} AND status = '1' AND type = #{type} AND status = '0' AND status IN ('0', '1')
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM orders
        <where>
            <choose>
                <when test="mode == 'active'">
                    <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
                </when>
                <when test="mode == 'dateRange'">
                    <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.dateRangeCondition" />
                </when>
                <otherwise>
                    <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.complexCommonCondition" />
                </otherwise>
            </choose>
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM orders AND status = '1' AND type = #{type} AND status = '0' AND status IN ('0', '1') AND id IN #{id}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM orders
        <where>
            <if test="filterEnabled">
                <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.complexCommonCondition" />
            </if>
            <if test="ids != null and ids.size() > 0">
                AND id IN
                <foreach collection="ids" item="id" open="(" separator="," close=")">#{id}</foreach>
            </if>
        </where>

            <if test="status != null">AND status = #{status}</if>
            <if test="type != null">AND type = #{type}</if>
        </where>
        LIMIT 1000

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = '1' AND type = 'VIP' AND status = '1' AND type = 'NORMAL' AND status = '1' AND status IN ('1', '2')

                        <when test="type == 'NORMAL'">AND status = '1' AND type = 'NORMAL'</when>
                        <otherwise>AND status = '1'</otherwise>

        </where>

sql: SELECT name, email, status, type id FROM users AND name = #{name}
templateSql:
SELECT
        <if test="includeName">name,</if>
        <if test="includeEmail">email,</if>
        <if test="includeStatus">status,</if>
        <if test="includeType">type</if>
        id
        FROM users
        <where><if test="name != null">AND name = #{name}</if></where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = #{status} AND type = #{type} AND name = #{name} AND status = #{status} AND type = #{type} AND status = #{status} AND type = #{type} AND name = #{name} AND type = #{type} 1=1
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <choose>
                <when test="status != null">
                    <choose>
                        <when test="type != null">
                            <choose>
                                <when test="name != null">AND status = #{status} AND type = #{type} AND name = #{name}</when>
                                <otherwise>AND status = #{status} AND type = #{type}</otherwise>
                            </choose>
                        </when>
                        <otherwise>AND status = #{status}</otherwise>
                    </choose>
                </when>
                <when test="type != null">
                    <choose>
                        <when test="name != null">AND type = #{type} AND name = #{name}</when>
                        <otherwise>AND type = #{type}</otherwise>
                    </choose>
                </when>
                <otherwise>1=1</otherwise>
            </choose>
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = #{status} AND type IN #{t} AND name LIKE CONCAT('%', #{name}, '%') AND id NOT IN #{id}

            </if>
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND name LIKE #{namePattern} AND email LIKE #{emailPattern} AND status = #{effectiveStatus}
templateSql:
<bind name="namePattern" value="'%' + name + '%'" />
        <bind name="emailPattern" value="'%' + email + '%'" />
        <bind name="effectiveStatus" value="status != null ? status : '1'" />
        SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <if test="name != null">AND name LIKE #{namePattern}</if>
            <if test="email != null">AND email LIKE #{emailPattern}</if>
            AND status = #{effectiveStatus}
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users status IN '1' '0' #{s}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <if test="statusList != null and statusList.size() > 0">
                status IN
                <foreach collection="statusList" item="s" open="(" separator="," close=")">
                    <choose>
                        <when test="s == 'active'">'1'</when>
                        <when test="s == 'inactive'">'0'</when>
                        <otherwise>#{s}</otherwise>
                    </choose>
                </foreach>
            </if>
        </where>

sql: INSERT INTO users (name, email, status, type) VALUES (#{u.name}, #{u.email}, #{u.status}'1', #{u.type}'NORMAL')

INSERT INTO users (name, email, status, type) VALUES
        <foreach collection="users" item="u" separator=",">
            (#{u.name}, #{u.email}, 
            <choose><when test="u.status != null">#{u.status}</when><otherwise>'1'</otherwise></choose>,
            <choose><when test="u.type != null">#{u.type}</when><otherwise>'NORMAL'</otherwise></choose>)
        </foreach>

sql: SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail, o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus, o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail,
               o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus,
               o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt
        FROM users u
        INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail, o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus, o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail,
               o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus,
               o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt
        FROM users u
        LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, oi.id as itemId, oi.product_id as productId, p.name as productName, oi.quantity, oi.price FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id INNER JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id INNER JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE u.id = #{userId}
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName,
               o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo,
               oi.id as itemId, oi.product_id as productId,
               p.name as productName, oi.quantity, oi.price
        FROM users u
        INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        INNER JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id
        INNER JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id
        WHERE u.id = #{userId}

sql: SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail, o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus, o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id AND u.id = #{userId} AND o.status = #{status} LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail,
               o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus,
               o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt
        FROM users u
        INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        <where>
            <if test="userId != null">AND u.id = #{userId}</if>
            <if test="status != null">AND o.status = #{status}</if>
        </where>
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id IN #{id}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        WHERE id IN
        <foreach collection="userIds" item="id" open="(" separator="," close=")">
            #{id}
        </foreach>

sql: SELECT * FROM users u WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id AND o.status = #{status} ) LIMIT 10
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) as orderCount FROM users u WHERE u.id IN (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM orders) LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName,
               (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) as orderCount
        FROM users u
        WHERE u.id IN (SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM orders)
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail, o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus, o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id AND u.id = #{userId} AND o.status = #{orderStatus} LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail,
               o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus,
               o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt
        FROM users u
        <if test="userId != null">
        INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        </if>
        <if test="userId == null">
        LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        </if>
        <where>
            <if test="userId != null">AND u.id = #{userId}</if>
            <if test="orderStatus != null">AND o.status = #{orderStatus}</if>
        </where>
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE type = #{type} UNION SELECT * FROM users WHERE status = '1'

SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail, o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus, o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt FROM users u INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.amount >= #{minAmount} AND o.user_id IN ( SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM orders) ) LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, u.email as userEmail,
               o.id as orderId, o.order_no as orderNo, o.status as orderStatus,
               o.amount, o.created_at as createdAt
        FROM users u
        INNER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        WHERE o.amount >= #{minAmount}
        AND o.user_id IN (
            SELECT user_id FROM orders 
            WHERE amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM orders)
        )
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0) FROM orders
templateSql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(amount), 0) FROM orders

sql: SELECT COALESCE(AVG(amount), 0) FROM orders
templateSql: SELECT COALESCE(AVG(amount), 0) FROM orders

sql: SELECT MAX(amount) as maxAmount, MIN(amount) as minAmount FROM orders
templateSql: SELECT MAX(amount) as maxAmount, MIN(amount) as minAmount FROM orders

sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM orders GROUP BY status
templateSql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM orders GROUP BY status

sql: SELECT user_id as userId, status, COUNT(*) as count FROM orders WHERE user_id = #{userId} GROUP BY user_id, status
templateSql:
SELECT user_id as userId, status, COUNT(*) as count 
        FROM orders 
        WHERE user_id = #{userId}
        GROUP BY user_id, status

sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM orders GROUP BY status HAVING COUNT(*) >= #{minCount}
templateSql:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count 
        FROM orders 
        GROUP BY status
        HAVING COUNT(*) >= #{minCount}

sql: SELECT id as orderId, order_no as orderNo, status, amount, CASE status WHEN 'completed' THEN '已完成' WHEN 'pending' THEN '待处理' WHEN 'processing' THEN '处理中' WHEN 'cancelled' THEN '已取消' ELSE '未知' END as statusLabel FROM orders LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT id as orderId, order_no as orderNo, status, amount,
               CASE status
                   WHEN 'completed' THEN '已完成'
                   WHEN 'pending' THEN '待处理'
                   WHEN 'processing' THEN '处理中'
                   WHEN 'cancelled' THEN '已取消'
                   ELSE '未知'
               END as statusLabel
        FROM orders
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT id as orderId, order_no as orderNo, created_at as createdAt, YEAR(created_at) as year, MONTH(created_at) as month, DAY(created_at) as day FROM orders WHERE YEAR(created_at) = #{year} LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT id as orderId, order_no as orderNo, created_at as createdAt,
               YEAR(created_at) as year,
               MONTH(created_at) as month,
               DAY(created_at) as day
        FROM orders
        WHERE YEAR(created_at) = #{year}
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE CONCAT(name, '-', email) LIKE CONCAT(#{prefix}, '%')
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        WHERE CONCAT(name, '-', email) LIKE CONCAT(#{prefix}, '%')

sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE SUBSTRING(email, 1, 3) = #{pattern}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        WHERE SUBSTRING(email, 1, 3) = #{pattern}

sql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN status = #{status} THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0) FROM orders
templateSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN status = #{status} THEN amount ELSE 0 END), 0) 
        FROM orders

sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count, SUM(amount) as sum, AVG(amount) as avg, MAX(amount) as max, MIN(amount) as min FROM orders GROUP BY status
templateSql:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count,
               SUM(amount) as sum,
               AVG(amount) as avg,
               MAX(amount) as max,
               MIN(amount) as min
        FROM orders
        GROUP BY status

sql: SELECT id as orderId, order_no as orderNo, amount, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o2 WHERE o2.amount > orders.amount) + 1 as `rank` FROM orders ORDER BY `rank` LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT id as orderId, order_no as orderNo, amount,
               (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o2 WHERE o2.amount > orders.amount) + 1 as `rank`
        FROM orders
        ORDER BY `rank`
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY id LIMIT #{limit}
templateSql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY id LIMIT #{limit}

sql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY id LIMIT #{limit} OFFSET #{offset}
templateSql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY id LIMIT #{limit} OFFSET #{offset}

sql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY id LIMIT #{pageSize} OFFSET #{offset}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY id
        <if test="offset != null and pageSize != null">
            LIMIT #{pageSize} OFFSET #{offset}
        </if>

sql: SELECT DISTINCT status FROM orders
templateSql: SELECT DISTINCT status FROM orders

sql: SELECT DISTINCT name, email FROM users WHERE status IS NOT NULL
templateSql: SELECT DISTINCT name, email FROM users WHERE status IS NOT NULL

sql: INSERT INTO users (id, name, email, status, type) VALUES (#{id}, #{name}, #{email}, #{status}, #{type}) ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE name = VALUES(name), email = VALUES(email), status = VALUES(status), type = VALUES(type)
templateSql:
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email, status, type)
        VALUES (#{id}, #{name}, #{email}, #{status}, #{type})
        ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
        name = VALUES(name), email = VALUES(email), status = VALUES(status), type = VALUES(type)

sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = 1
templateSql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = 1

sql: SELECT DISTINCT status FROM orders AND status = #{status} LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT DISTINCT status FROM orders
        <where>
            <if test="status != null">AND status = #{status}</if>
        </where>
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM orders AND status = #{status}
templateSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM orders
        <where>
            <if test="status != null">AND status = #{status}</if>
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY status, amount DESC LIMIT #{pageSize} OFFSET #{offset}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY status, amount DESC
        <if test="offset != null and pageSize != null">
            LIMIT #{pageSize} OFFSET #{offset}
        </if>

sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM orders WHERE amount > #{minAmount} GROUP BY status HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 ORDER BY count DESC LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count FROM orders
        WHERE amount > #{minAmount}
        GROUP BY status
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 0
        ORDER BY count DESC
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT * FROM users WHERE type = #{type} UNION SELECT * FROM users WHERE status = '1' LIMIT #{limit}
SELECT id, name, email, status, type FROM users AND status = #{status}
templateSql:
SELECT <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.userBaseColumns" />
        FROM users
        <where>
            <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
        </where>

sql: SELECT id, name, email, status, type, created_at, updated_at FROM users AND name LIKE CONCAT('%', #{name}, '%') AND status = #{status}
templateSql:
SELECT <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.userBaseColumns" />,
               <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.auditColumns" />
        FROM users
        <where>
            <if test="name != null">AND name LIKE CONCAT('%', #{name}, '%')</if>
            <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = #{status} AND created_at >= #{startDate} AND created_at <= #{endDate} AND type = #{type}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <include refid="userSearchWithCommon" />
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = #{status} AND status = '1' AND type = #{type} AND status = '0' AND status IN ('0', '1') AND created_at >= #{startDate} AND created_at <= #{endDate}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <choose>
                <when test="filterMode == 'active'">
                    <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
                </when>
                <when test="filterMode == 'complex'">
                    <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.complexCommonCondition" />
                </when>
                <otherwise>
                    <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.dateRangeCondition" />
                </otherwise>
            </choose>
        </where>

sql: SELECT id, name, email, status, type FROM users AND id IN #{id} AND status = #{status} LIMIT #{pageSize} OFFSET #{offset}
templateSql:
SELECT <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.userBaseColumns" />
        FROM users
        <where>
            <if test="ids != null and ids.size() > 0">
                AND id IN
                <foreach collection="ids" item="id" open="(" separator="," close=")">#{id}</foreach>
            </if>
            <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
        </where>
        <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.paginationCondition" />

sql: SELECT u1.id, u1.name, u1.email, u1.status, u1.type FROM users u1 INNER JOIN users u2 ON u1.type = u2.type u1.status = #{status} AND u2.status = u1.status
templateSql:
SELECT u1.id, u1.name, u1.email, u1.status, u1.type
        FROM users u1
        INNER JOIN users u2 ON u1.type = u2.type
        <where>
            u1.status = #{status}
            AND u2.status = u1.status
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = '1' AND type = #{type} AND status = '0' AND status IN ('0', '1')
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <if test="useComplex">
                <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.complexCommonCondition" />
            </if>
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = #{status} AND type = #{type}
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <trim prefix="WHERE " prefixOverrides="AND|OR">
            <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
            <if test="type != null">AND type = #{type}</if>
        </trim>

sql: UPDATE users status = #{status}, updated_at = NOW() AND status = #{status}
templateSql:
UPDATE users
        <set>
            <if test="status != null">status = #{status},</if>
            updated_at = NOW()
        </set>
        <where>
            <include refid="com.test.mapper.CommonMapper.activeStatusCondition" />
        </where>

sql: SELECT * FROM users AND status = '1' AND type = #{type} AND status = '0' AND status IN ('0', '1') AND type IS NOT NULL
templateSql:
SELECT * FROM users
        <where>
            <include refid="chainedCondition" />
        </where>

sql: SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id), 0) + 1 FROM users INSERT INTO users (id, name, email, status) VALUES (#{id}, #{name}, #{email}, '1')
templateSql:
<selectKey keyProperty="id" resultType="integer" order="BEFORE">
            SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id), 0) + 1 FROM users
        </selectKey>
        INSERT INTO users (id, name, email, status) 
        VALUES (#{id}, #{name}, #{email}, '1')

sql: SELECT FLOOR(RAND() * 1000000) INSERT INTO users (id, name, email, status) VALUES (#{id}, #{name}, #{email}, '1')

        VALUES (#{id}, #{name}, #{email}, '1')

sql: SELECT u.id as u_id, u.name as u_name, u.email as u_email, o.id as o_id, o.order_no as o_no, o.amount as o_amount FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id LIMIT 10
templateSql:
SELECT u.id as u_id, u.name as u_name, u.email as u_email,
               o.id as o_id, o.order_no as o_no, o.amount as o_amount
        FROM users u
        LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        LIMIT 10

sql: SELECT u.id as u_id, u.name as u_name, o.id as o_id, o.order_no as o_no, o.amount as o_amount FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE u.id = 1
templateSql:

               o.id as o_id, o.order_no as o_no, o.amount as o_amount
        FROM users u
        LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
        WHERE u.id = 1

sql: SELECT u.id as userId, u.name as userName, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id) as orderCount FROM users u LIMIT 10
